records.ety: [L. post behind, after; cf. Skr. paçcābehind, afterwards.]

defs: A prefix signifying behind, back, after; as, postcommissure, postdot, postscript.

records.ety: [F. aposter to place in a post or position, generally for a bad purpose.]

defs: Hired to do what is wrong; suborned.

records.ety: [AS., fr. L. postis, akin to ponere, positum, to place. See Position, and cf. 4th Post.]

defs: A piece of timber, metal, or other solid substance, fixed, or to be fixed, firmly in an upright position, especially when intended as a stay or support to something else; a pillar; as, a hitching post; a fence post; the posts of a house.
quotes: They shall take of the blood, and strike it on the two side posts and on the upper doorpost of the houses.
Then by main force pulled up, and on his shoulders bore, The gates of Azza, post and massy bar.
Unto his order he was a noble post.
notes: ☞ Post, in the sense of an upright timber or strut, is used in composition, in such words as king-post, queen-post, crown-post, gatepost, etc.

defs: The doorpost of a victualer's shop or inn, on which were chalked the scores of customers; hence, a score; a debt.
quotes: When God sends coin I will discharge your post.

records.ety: [F. poste, LL. posta station, post (where horses were kept), properly, a fixed or set place, fem. fr. L. positus placed, p. p. of ponere. See Position, and cf. Post a pillar.]

defs: The place at which anything is stopped, placed, or fixed; a station.
A station, or one of a series of stations, established for the refreshment and accommodation of travelers on some recognized route; as, a stage or railway post.
A military station; the place at which a soldier or a body of troops is stationed; also, the troops at such a station.
The piece of ground to which a sentinel's walk is limited.
A messenger who goes from station; an express; especially, one who is employed by the government to carry letters and parcels regularly from one place to another; a letter carrier; a postman.
quotes: In certain places there be always fresh posts, to carry that further which is brought unto them by the other.
I fear my Julia would not deign my lines, Receiving them from such a worthless post.

defs: An established conveyance for letters from one place or station to another; especially, the governmental system in any country for carrying and distributing letters and parcels; the post office; the mail; hence, the carriage by which the mail is transported.
quotes: I send you the fair copy of the poem on dullness, which I should not care to hazard by the common post.

defs: Haste or speed, like that of a messenger or mail carrier.
One who has charge of a station, especially of a postal station.
quotes: He held office of postmaster, or, as it was then called, post, for several years.

defs: A station, office, or position of service, trust, or emolument; as, the post of duty; the post of danger.
quotes: The post of honor is a private station.

defs: A size of printing and writing paper. See the Table under Paper.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Posted; p. pr. & vb. n. Posting.]

defs: To attach to a post, a wall, or other usual place of affixing public notices; to placard; as, to post a notice; to post playbills.
notes: ☞ Formerly, a large post was erected before the sheriff's office, or in some public place, upon which legal notices were displayed. This way of advertisement has not entirely gone of use.

defs: To hold up to public blame or reproach; to advertise opprobriously; to denounce by public proclamation; as, to post one for cowardice.
quotes: On pain of being posted to your sorrow Fail not, at four, to meet me.

defs: To enter (a name) on a list, as for service, promotion, or the like.
To assign to a station; to set; to place; as, to post a sentinel.
To carry, as an account, from the journal to the ledger; as, to post an account; to transfer, as accounts, to the ledger.
quotes: You have not posted your books these ten years.

defs: To place in the care of the post; to mail; as, to post a letter.
To inform; to give the news to; to make (one) acquainted with the details of a subject; -- often with up.
quotes: Thoroughly posted up in the politics and literature of the day.

defs: To travel with post horses; figuratively, to travel in haste.
quotes: And post o'er land and ocean without rest.

defs: To rise and sink in the saddle, in accordance with the motion of the horse, esp. in trotting.
With post horses; hence, in haste; as, to travel post.
